overview: Build a local professional AI canvas using tldraw, Hono, SQLite, and GPT Image 2. Ralph must execute one story per iteration, commit story changes, and keep secrets out of the repository.
goals: Provide a local tldraw-based canvas for AI image creation and visual comparison.
Support GPT Image 2 text-to-image as the primary workflow.
Support single selected canvas image as a reference for image-to-image generation.
Persist projects, history, generated image metadata, and assets locally.
Offer Docker Compose as a one-command production-style local runtime.
nonGoals: Do not support models other than gpt-image-2 in v1.
Do not implement user accounts, cloud sync, billing, multi-project management, or collaborative editing.
Do not implement mask-based inpainting or local region editing.
Do not implement arbitrary third-party request/response mapping; only support OpenAI-compatible base URLs.
Do not commit real API keys, generated assets, SQLite databases, or Ralph runtime state.
successMetrics: A user can open the local app, type a prompt, choose a size, and generate images onto the tldraw canvas.
Generated images are written under the local data directory and remain available after restart.
The canvas snapshot and generation history restore after restart.
A selected canvas image can be used as a single reference image for a new generation.
Docker Compose can build and serve the app from one localhost port.
openQuestions: No product-level open questions remain. If live API credentials are missing, record that the final live API smoke could not run instead of committing secrets.

stack.frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript + tldraw + Tailwind CSS + lucide-react
stack.database: SQLite using Drizzle ORM and better-sqlite3
stack.imageProvider: OpenAI-compatible Images API, fixed model gpt-image-2
stack.deploy: Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml with a single app service

routes.name: Canvas App
routes.purpose: React/tldraw creative workspace served by Vite in development and by Hono in production.

routes.name: Health Check
routes.purpose: Confirm the API server is running.

routes.name: App Config
routes.purpose: Return fixed model, supported size presets, style presets, quality options, formats, and counts.

routes.name: Project State
routes.purpose: Load and save the single default project and tldraw snapshot.

routes.name: Text To Image
routes.purpose: Generate one or more GPT Image 2 images from a prompt.

routes.name: Reference Image Generation
routes.purpose: Generate images using a single selected canvas image as reference.

routes.name: Asset File
routes.purpose: Serve locally stored generated image files.

uiNotes: The app opens directly into the working canvas; do not build a marketing landing page.
Use a full-height tldraw canvas with a fixed right-side AI panel.
Keep the canvas clean: do not place prompt labels next to generated images.
Use scene-oriented size presets with visible dimensions, plus custom width/height inputs.
Hide background and transparency controls because v1 only supports gpt-image-2.
Basic controls stay visible: prompt, style preset, size preset/custom size, count, generate/cancel.
Advanced controls are collapsible: quality and output format.
History shows recent generations with actions to locate, rerun, and download.

importFormat.description: Not applicable for v1. Users create and persist work locally inside the app.

rules: Ralph must complete exactly one story per iteration and commit story changes unless explicitly run with no-commit.
Treat the current uncommitted scaffold files as draft work that US-001 should normalize, not as final implementation.
The only supported image model is gpt-image-2. Do not add model switching UI.
Read OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_BASE_URL, PORT, HOST, and DATA_DIR from environment variables or .env.
Do not read the user's global Codex config for image API credentials.
Do not log or commit real API keys.
The OpenAI-compatible base URL is configurable, but request and response semantics remain OpenAI Images API-compatible.
Validate custom sizes on both frontend and backend. Reject invalid values with clear Chinese errors instead of silently changing them.
Batch generation supports counts 1, 2, and 4 with backend concurrency limited to 2. Do not restrict batch count for large images.
On partial batch failures, keep successful assets and record failed outputs.
Canceling a generation aborts the client request and prevents canceled results from being inserted into the canvas.
Deleting an image shape from the canvas does not delete its local file or history entry.
The single default project autosaves with debounce.
Docker Compose uses one app service and bind-mounts ./data to /app/data.
Every story must run pnpm typecheck and pnpm build after implementation. Frontend stories must also perform browser verification.

stories.title: Standardize repository scaffold for Ralph execution
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a clean pnpm workspace, Ralph task file, and operational repository notes so subsequent Ralph stories can run deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: Normalize the existing draft scaffold into a valid pnpm workspace with apps/api, apps/web, and packages/shared.
Install or preserve Ralph templates under .agents/ralph and keep this PRD at .agents/tasks/prd-gpt-image-canvas.json.
Root scripts include dev, api:dev, web:dev, typecheck, build, and start.
.env.example documents OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_BASE_URL, OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL=gpt-image-2, PORT, HOST, and DATA_DIR without real secrets.
.gitignore excludes .env, .ralph, data, generated assets, databases, node_modules, and build outputs.
AGENTS.md documents pnpm, quality gates, app boundaries, and the no-secret rule.
Example: running pnpm --version reports pnpm 9.x and the workspace files are present.
Negative case: real API keys must not appear in README, .env.example, PRD, AGENTS.md, or committed files.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build either pass or fail only because dependencies have not yet been installed; if dependencies are missing, run pnpm install and rerun.

stories.title: Build Hono API with SQLite persistence
stories.description: As a local app user, I want the API to persist my single project, generation history, and generated asset metadata in SQLite so work survives restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add apps/api with Hono, @hono/node-server, dotenv, drizzle-orm, drizzle-kit, and better-sqlite3.
Create SQLite schema for Project, Asset, GenerationRecord, and GenerationOutput.
Initialize DATA_DIR, assets directory, and database file automatically at startup.
Implement GET /api/health returning ok status.
Implement GET /api/config returning model gpt-image-2, style presets, size presets, qualities, formats, and counts.
Implement GET /api/project and PUT /api/project for the single default project snapshot.
Example: a saved snapshot can be loaded again after restarting the API.
Negative case: invalid project payload returns a 400 JSON error and does not corrupt stored state.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Create React tldraw workspace shell
stories.description: As a creator, I want a Chinese professional canvas interface with tldraw and an AI control panel so I can start composing images immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Add apps/web with React, Vite, TypeScript, tldraw, Tailwind CSS, lucide-react, and a Vite proxy to the API.
Render a full-height tldraw canvas with a fixed right-side Chinese AI panel.
Panel includes visible prompt, style preset, scene size preset, custom width/height, count, generate, and cancel controls.
Panel includes collapsible advanced controls for quality and output format.
Do not show model selection, background controls, or transparent background controls.
Example: selecting a scene preset updates the visible width and height.
Negative case: an empty prompt disables generation or shows a clear Chinese validation message.
Browser verification: run the app, load the workspace, confirm tldraw canvas and panel render without overlap.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Autosave and restore the tldraw project
stories.description: As a creator, I want my canvas to autosave and restore so refreshing the app does not lose layout or work in progress.
acceptanceCriteria: On app load, fetch /api/project and restore the saved tldraw snapshot if present.
Autosave the tldraw snapshot through PUT /api/project with debounce after canvas changes.
Show a compact save status in the AI panel or app chrome.
Do not save on every pointer movement without debounce.
Example: draw or add a shape, refresh, and the shape reappears.
Negative case: if the API save fails, the UI shows a non-blocking Chinese error and keeps the current canvas intact.
Browser verification: create a shape, refresh, and confirm it restores.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Implement GPT Image 2 provider and size validation
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a safe GPT Image 2 provider abstraction so generation requests are validated and secrets remain on the server.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement an image provider interface for generate and edit operations, with only an OpenAI-compatible GPT Image 2 implementation.
Read OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_BASE_URL from environment variables or .env; do not read global Codex config.
Hard-code or enforce model gpt-image-2 on provider calls.
Implement shared frontend/backend size validation for scene presets and custom sizes.
Return Chinese JSON errors for missing API key, invalid prompt, invalid size, unsupported provider behavior, or upstream failure.
Do not log API keys or full authorization headers.
Example: valid custom size passes validation and produces the OpenAI-compatible size value.
Negative case: invalid custom size returns 400 before contacting upstream.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Generate text-to-image assets onto the canvas
stories.description: As a creator, I want to generate one or more images from a text prompt and place them on the canvas in a grid so I can compare options visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement POST /api/images/generate accepting prompt, presetId, size, quality, outputFormat, and count.
Compose style presets into the effective prompt by appending the preset prompt transparently.
Execute batch requests with backend concurrency limited to 2.
Save successful image files under data/assets and metadata/history in SQLite.
Return a generation record with per-output success or failure.
Insert successful image outputs into tldraw as image assets and shapes in a neat grid centered in the current viewport.
Canceling a request prevents canceled results from being inserted into the canvas.
Example: count 4 produces up to four canvas images arranged as a grid.
Negative case: one failed output still leaves successful outputs on the canvas and records the failure.
Browser verification: submit a generation request using configured credentials and confirm image insertion when credentials are available; otherwise verify the missing-key error.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Generate from a single selected reference image
stories.description: As a creator, I want to select one image on the canvas and use it as a reference for a new GPT Image 2 generation.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect when exactly one tldraw image shape is selected and expose it as the current reference in the AI panel.
Implement POST /api/images/edit accepting prompt, size, quality, outputFormat, count, and one reference image.
Support reference images from generated assets and from user-added canvas image assets when their data can be read by the frontend.
Place edit outputs near the current viewport in the same grid style as text-to-image outputs.
Record referenceAssetId when the reference maps to a locally stored asset.
Example: selecting one image enables the reference generation mode and produces a new related image.
Negative case: selecting zero, multiple, or non-image shapes keeps reference generation disabled with a clear Chinese hint.
Browser verification: select one canvas image and confirm reference UI state; perform live edit smoke only when credentials support it.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Add generation history actions
stories.description: As a creator, I want a simple persistent history so I can locate, rerun, and download previous generation results.
acceptanceCriteria: Show recent generation records in the right panel with prompt excerpt, mode, size, status, created time, and output count.
Clicking locate moves the tldraw camera to the corresponding canvas image when it still exists.
Rerun submits a new generation with the same prompt, preset, size, quality, format, count, and reference when available.
Download opens or downloads the original locally stored asset through /api/assets/:id/download.
Deleting a canvas image shape does not delete the asset file or history record.
Example: after refresh, history still appears and download works for stored assets.
Negative case: locating a deleted canvas shape shows a Chinese not-found message instead of crashing.
Browser verification: generate or use existing history, locate an output, rerun it, and download an asset.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Package the app with Docker Compose
stories.description: As a local operator, I want Docker Compose to build and serve the complete app from one localhost port with data persisted on the host.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Dockerfile using Node.js 22 and pnpm via Corepack.
Build shared, web, and API packages in the image.
Serve the built web bundle through Hono from the same app service that serves /api.
Add docker-compose.yml with one app service, localhost port mapping, env_file .env, and ./data bind-mounted to /app/data.
Document Docker startup in README.
Example: docker compose up --build serves the app at localhost on the configured port.
Negative case: missing .env credentials does not stop the app from booting; generation endpoints return missing-key errors.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass; docker compose config succeeds.

stories.title: Run final local, Docker, and live API smoke verification
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want final end-to-end verification so the Ralph build leaves a usable local professional image canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Run pnpm typecheck and pnpm build successfully.
Start the local dev workflow and browser-verify the canvas, AI panel, autosave, history, and download flows.
Run docker compose config and, when Docker is available, docker compose up --build long enough to confirm the app boots.
If .env has valid credentials, perform one live text-to-image smoke and one live reference-image smoke using gpt-image-2.
If credentials or provider capability are unavailable, record the exact reason in Ralph progress without committing secrets.
Update README with final setup, dev, Docker, data storage, and troubleshooting notes.
Example: a user following README can run the app and generate onto the canvas.
Negative case: missing credentials are handled as a runtime configuration problem, not as a build failure.
Verification: final story records commands run, browser findings, Docker findings, and live API smoke status.
